text.title: Webinar Planning Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Webinar Planning application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Promotion MUST begin ≥3 weeks before the webinar date. Sub-3-week promos are rejected.
rule.rationale: Webinar registration follows a multi-touch curve; < 3 weeks under-fills.

rule.statement: Plan MUST name the format (live demo / panel / interview / training) before content outline. Unnamed-format planning is rejected.
rule.rationale: Format determines content shape, promo angle, and required prep; choosing later forces rework.

rule.statement: Promo MUST cover ≥3 channels (email, social, partners). Single-channel promo is rejected.
rule.rationale: Single-channel promos under-fill by 40-70% versus the same total spend distributed across 3.

rule.statement: Registration page MUST contain: outcome, presenter bio, agenda, social proof, single signup form. Pages missing any element are rejected.
rule.rationale: Each missing element costs registration; the checklist is the conversion floor.

rule.statement: Slide outline MUST be locked ≥2 weeks before delivery. Last-minute rebuilds are rejected.
rule.rationale: Late rebuilds collapse rehearsal time and produce stage panic; the outline freeze guards delivery quality.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
